element_introduction_order.first_appears: MB Time Travel Research Centre (hub level)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player begins each era unarmed; melee is foundational

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Caribbean 1650 AD or Western United States 1860 AD
France 1943 AD or final boss arena

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Four orbs per era level; fifth from boss defeat. Required to power time machine
Boss defeat yields fifth orb; culminates in Drakkon King battle in present
Persistent pressure mechanic; one day present = six seconds gameplay

typical_relief_triggers: Acquiring golden orbs from enemy drops
Reaching safe areas between combat encounters
Defeating Drakkon Lord boss and obtaining fifth orb
typical_tension_triggers: Approaching new era with fresh unarmed state
Time limit pressure increasing as deadline approaches
Enemy encounters in unfamiliar historical settings
Golden orb collection pressure (must find/earn all five)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Sustained pressure pacing with escalating weaponry availability. Global time limit creates constant low-level tension that intensifies toward endgame. Relief comes through weapon acquisition and orb collection milestones.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Medieval England introduces core mechanics (jumping, punching, basic sword combat) with minimal hazards. Player learns unarmed-to-armed progression pattern.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Caribbean and Western United States eras introduce ranged weapons (throwing knives, firearms) and more aggressive enemy patterns. Golden orb hunting becomes more demanding.
difficulty_curve.late_game: France 1943 AD introduces grenades and laser weapons. Final boss arena (Drakkon King) requires mastery of all mechanics. Time limit pressure peaks near 36-minute mark.
notable_spikes: First Drakkon Lord boss encounter (Medieval England) - establishes boss pattern
Final Drakkon King battle in present (requires all collected orbs and weapons mastery)
Time limit approaching zero (psychological spike; no continues available)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of four historical eras (1250, 1650, 1860, 1943) follows identical structure: hub entry → weapon acquisition → golden orb collection (4 scattered) → Drakkon Lord boss battle → orb #5 acquisition → return to present time.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Medieval England 1250 AD serves as template for all subsequent eras

notable_level_patterns.description: MB Time Travel Research Centre acts as central hub. Player departs to four disconnected era levels, must return to hub after each successful orb collection before traveling to next era.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: MB Time Travel Research Centre (hub) with five spokes to Medieval England, Caribbean, Western United States, France, and Final Battle

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level begins with player unarmed. Gift-wrapped packages distributed throughout level provide era-appropriate weapons. Escalation follows historical progression: swords → knives/guns → grenades/lasers.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: France 1943 AD features most advanced weapons (grenades, lasers) as reward for reaching late game
